def: s.m. = pāshaṇḍ, q.v. s.m. Heresy, heterodoxy, false doctrine; blasphemy, wickedness; deceit in religious matters, hypocrisy, dissimulation, pretence, disguise; — heretic, &c. = pāshanḍī, q.v.
subs: pākhand karnā, pākhand maćānā, v.n. To practise dissimulation, to play the hypocrite, to feign, pretend.

def: s.m. Heresy, heterodoxy, false doctrine; blasphemy, wickedness; deceit in religious matters, hypocrisy, dissimulation, pretence, disguise; — heretic, &c. = pāshanḍī, q.v.
subs: pākhand karnā, pākhand maćānā, v.n. To practise dissimulation, to play the hypocrite, to feign, pretend.

def: adj. & s.m. Dissembling, hypocritical, deceitful, sanctimonious; — heretic, heterodox Hindū, religious impostor, hypocrite; sectarian, Jain, Buddhist, one who adopts the external marks or characteristics of a tribe or sect but does not conform to the orthodox tenets of the Hindū faith nor respect the ordinances of the Vedas.

s.m. A long piercing instrument with which builders make holes in walls; a mason's chisel; a woollen cloth, or a plank prepared with a piece of wood at each end, on which bricks, clay, &c. are drawn; — oppression, tyranny; anguish, affliction; exuding (of water, &c.), exudation; — name of the father and of the son of Afrāsyāb; name of a warrior of Irān.
s.f. Small stone used as a weight; — stone-cutter's chisel; mason's hammer.
adj. = pāshāṇ-may, q.v.s.v. pāshāṇ.
v.t. = پکهارنا pakhārnā, q.v. v.t. To wash, to rinse, to clean.
s.f. = pakhāwaj, q.v. s.f. A kind of drum, tabor, timbrel.
adj. Penitent, sorry, repentant, remorseful, filled with regret; abashed, ashamed; disgraced
subs: (-se), To be sorry (for), to repent, regret; to be or feel ashamed (of).

def: s.f. Regret, repentance, contrition, penitence; shame; disgrace.
s.f. Scattering, sprinkling (used in comp. e.g. āb-pāshī, q.v.).
s.m. Hair, wool, fur, down; the pubes; anything insignificant or of no moment, anything worthless
subs: v.t. To be perfectly contented and independent of; to despise, disregard
v.t. To regard as insignificant, &c., to despise
v.n. 'Not a hair to be pulled out'; to suffer nothing from the enmity of another, to be unruffled (said in great contempt of an adversary).

def: s.m. (?), A kind of sweetmeat.
adj. Woolly hairy; woollen.
adj. Woolly hairy; woollen.
adj. = pashmīn, q.v.; — s.m. Woollen cloth. adj. Woolly hairy; woollen.

ety: Pehl. pāshnak; Zend pāshna; S. pārshṇi, fr. sphur
def: s.m. Heel; pastern (of a horse).
s.m. A gnat.
s.m. (Old. H.) = يکهوارا pakhwārā, q.v.
s.f. Cord or rope for fastening the legs of a horse or other tame animal; a net.

s.m. One who casts a net, or snare, or noose; a fowler, bird-catcher; a member of a caste whose occupation is to extract the juice of the tāṛ palm (so called from their climbing with the aid of a noose or loop round their feet).
s.f. Reservoir of water for cattle in a season of drought.
s.m. One armed with a noose or net, &c. = pāsī, q.v. s.f. Cord or rope for fastening the legs of a horse or other tame animal; a net.
s.m. Watchman, sentinel ( = pās-bān, q.v.s.v. pās).
adj. & s.m. Ready, prepared; — preparation for a journey; provision for a journey.

ety: pasīj˚ = Prk. पसिज्ज˚; S. प्रस्विद्य(ति), rt. स्विद with प्र
def: v.n. To perspire, to sweat; to exude; to melt, dissolve; to melt (with pity, &c.), to soften, feel sorrow or pity (for), be touched (in this sense usually constructed with the word dil or one of its syn., e.g. mujhe kar ẕabḥ khanjar-se terā dil gar pasījā hai); to warm (towards one); — to be dressed over a slow fire, to simmer.
adj. Posterior, last, hindmost; newest; modern (see pasīnī).

ety: S. प्रस्विन्र+कः, rt. स्विद् with प्र
def: s.m. Perspiration, sweat; exudation
subs: v.n. To perspire, sweat; to perspire (through shame, &c.)
v.n. To be covered with sweat (through heat, or toil); to break into a violent perspiration (through shame, &c.); to be disturbed or alarmed, &c. ( = āb-āb honā, q.v.).

def: adj. Modern, recent; — pasīniyāṅ, s.m. The moderns.
part. Tied, bound, fettered.
s.m. = past-qad, q.v.s.v. past.

ety: S. पर्शु+dim. aff. lī = fem. fr. S. aff. रः
def: s.f. A rib; the præcordia
subs: v.n. To feel a quivering or thrilling sensation over the ribs (regarded as a sign of an impending visit from an absent friend, or as a perception of his or her condition)
s.m. Side; a tract of country.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀁𑀲𑀼𑀮𑀺𑀆 (paṃsuliā), from Sanskrit पर्शु (parśu), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *párćuš, from Proto-Indo-European *pérḱus.

def: v.n. To have a flow of milk (an animal); the milk to descend (to the udder).
adj. See s.v. pas for compounds of which that word is the first member.
adj. Low, humble; abject, mean, base, vile; lower; below
subs: adj. Of inferior understanding; of a mean disposition; low-natured; base, vile
adj. & s.m. Low in stature; a dwarf
v.t. To lower, put down; to render abject, to humiliate; to defeat, vanquish
v.n. To look above and below; to look about (one); to consider and take precautions against the vicissitudes of fortune
adj. Low-minded; mean-spirited; without courage; unambitious
v.n. To be humbled, be put down; to be humiliated; to be overcome, be vanquished.

def: adj. Past, former, old, ancient, of yore.
v.n. To regret, &c. = paćhtānā, q.v. v.n. To regret, repent, rue, grieve.
corr. of paćhtānā, q.v. v.n. To regret, repent, rue, grieve.
s.f. Lowness, inferiority; humility; baseness.
s.m. = پشو paśu, q.v.
— see پشو paśu.

s.m. Cattle, brute, beast, animal (neat-cattle, horses, goats, sheep, asses, and dogs); a young buffalo (local); an animal to be immolated, a victim; a mere animal in sacred things, an uninitiated person; a simpleton, blockhead; — a subordinate deity and one of Śiva's followers; — the soul (according to the tenets of Pāśupatas)
subs: paśu-pālak, s.m. Keeper of herds, herdsman, cattle-rearer
s.m. 'Lord of the animals,' or 'lord of a servant named Paśu', an epithet of Śiva; — paśu-pati-nāth, s.m. An epithet of Śivā
adj. Like an animal; brutish; uncivilized
s.m. A fragrant grass, Cyperus rotundus (which grows in pools frequented by cattle)
s.m. The characteristics of cattle, action or function of animals, treatment of cattle or animals; promiscuous cohabitation; the marrying of widows
s.m. 'Monarch of the beasts,' a lion
s.m. Cord for tethering cattle, tether for animals
s.m. The hair of an animal
s.f. The act of animal sacrifice; acting like cattle, copulation
s.f. A parody of the holy verse of the Vedas whispered in the ear of an animal about to be sacrificed
adj. & adv. Animal-like, brutish, irrational; — like an animal, brutally; — paśu-vad-gāmī, vulg. paśu-van-gāmī, adj. Walking like a brute beast
s.f. The fruit of the Spondias mangifera, the hog-plum.

def: s.f. The first stitching or basting (of a garment); running stitch; running and felling.

ety: S. प्र+सूचनीयं; or pasūj˚ = Prk. पसुज्ज˚ = S. प्रसीव्य˚, rt. प्रसिव्
def: v.t. To sew with a running stitch, to run; to baste.
s.f. Any small animal.
s.m. See s.v. پشو paśu.
s.f. = paśutva, q.v. s.m. The condition or nature of an animal, bestiality, brutality; the condition of an animal about to be immolated.
s.m. The condition or nature of an animal, bestiality, brutality; the condition of an animal about to be immolated.
wety: Borrowed from Pashto پښتو (paẍto), likely influenced by Persian پشتو (paštu).

def: s.m. Falling; sin, wickedness.
s.m. Flying, mode of flying, flight; throwing oneself into or down, falling, fall, downfall.
s.m. Leaf (of a tree, book, &c.) draught or cheque on a banker; any thin leaf or plate of metal; an ornament worn in the upper part of the ear
subs: v.n. Leaves to fall (in autumn); (met.) to have one's power or endurance exhausted; to be reduced to destitution or misery; to have one's patience tried or exhausted (by the scorn of a beloved person)
s.f. Statement of the assets and liabilities of an estate
pāt-kr̤im or kr̤imi, s.m. lit. 'Leaf-worm'; caterpillar.

ety: Zend pātar; S. pātar?; cf. pād-shāh
def: s.m. Throne.
s.m. = pāṭh, q.v. s.m. Water ( = jal).

s.m. Slab, tablet, board, plank, shutter; fold or leaf (of a door); throne, seat, stool; one of two millstones (either the upper or the lower); the stone or plank on which Indian washermen beat clothes; — bed or plot in a garden or field, terrace; one part, side, or edge of two pieces of cloth; folding clothes or laying them in order; — earthen hoop or brace to prevent a well from falling in; the horizontal beam to which the bullocks are attached in a sugar or oil mill, also the place where the driver sits; sack or load of corn; — silk cloth; cocoon of silk; — the plant Corchorus olitorius (from the fibres of which sacking and cordage are made); tow
subs: (S. paṭṭa+ambara), s.m. Silk cloth; silk garment
pāṭ mahishī, s.f. The queen who is installed or consecrated with the king, principal wife of a rājā, queen-consort

def: s.f. Good name, honour, character, reputation, credit (syn. ābrū)
subs: (-kī), To drag down (one's) good name, &c., to ruin the character, &c. (of), to disgrace
v.n. To lose one's good name, &c.; to be discredited, dishonoured, or disgraced

def: adj. Lying flat, lying on the face, upside down, overturned, upset; prone, inclined; falling flat, useless, ineffective (as the blow of a sword, &c.); lying uncultivated, fallow, waste (land); uninhabited, deserted (village); lying neglected, forgotten, effaced, wiped out; — s.m. A willing catamite
subs: (paṭ+par, or paṭ+paṛ, rt. of paṛnā), adj. & s.m. Level, flat; bare of trees, &c.; a bare plain, a desert waste; moist land caked by sunshine after rain or a flood
s.m. A flat surface; a bare plain, &c. ( = paṭpaṛ); flatness, evenness

def: s.m. Sound of falling, or of breaking or of beating; — adv. Quickly, instantly, at once, in a trice
subs: paṭ-paṭ (S. paṭa-paṭa), s.m. Continuous sound of beating, or of falling rain, &c.; — adv. Continuously, without ceasing
s.m. A beating; — paṭ-paṭ denā, v.t. To give (a child) a beating.

def: s.m. Cloth, coarse cloth or canvas; piece of cloth; fold of cloth; covering, screen, veil; a chequered cloth or board (on which chess, &c. is played); chair, throne; turban, tiara (in this sense it occurs in comp., as paṭ-rānī); a royal grant engraved on copper or stone, &c.
subs: paṭ-rānī, s.f. A queen (decorated with the tiara), the principal wife of a rājā or king ( = pāṭ-rānī)
(-kā), To open, or to remove, a screen; to take off or remove a covering; to lift, or to remove, a veil
s.m. The kind of cloth called paṭ.

def: s.m. Leaf of a door; valve of a folding door; door, shutter
subs: paṭ uṛaknā, paṭ band karnā, paṭ bheṛnā, paṭ lagānā, paṭ mārnā, v.n. To close a door

def: s.m. Breadth, expanse, extension; width or breadth (of cloth, of a river, &c.); (in Geom.) the intersection of a produced side of a figure and a perpendicular on it, or the figure formed by such intersection.
s.m. A leaf (used chiefly as first member of comp.)
subs: pat-jhaṛ, adj. & s.f. With the leaves dropped off, leafless; — fall of the leaf, autumn; — pat-jhaṛ honā, v.n. To lose its leaves in autumn (a tree); (met.) to decay (said of an aged person)
s.f. The fall of the leaf, autumn.

def: s.m. Leaf = pāt; pattā; q.v. s.m. Falling; sin, wickedness.
adj. & s.m. = pātr̤i, q.v.
part. adj. Watched, protected, preserved.
s.m. Plank, board, bench; slab or plank on which washermen beat clothes ( = pāṭ).

s.m. Sign, mark, signal, symptom, token, trace, clue, hint, direction, specification, particular mention; address (of a person), address (or place to which one is directed), label; book of instructions and regulations (for rent-collectors, &c.)
subs: (-kā), To obtain a clue (to), to trace, &c. = patā lagānā, q.v.
(-kā), To give the clue, or direction, or address (of); — to direct, point out
(-kā), To trace out, find out, or discover (the personality, abode, business, &c. of a person); to follow up a clue; to search, search out.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit पत्तिअ (pattia), from Sanskrit प्रत्याययति (pratyāyayati, “convinces”).

def: s.m. A straight, long, and broad sword with two edges; a wooden sword or foil (used in fencing, &c.), a cudgel; a board on which Hindūs sit whilst eating their meals or performing religious ceremonies ( = paṭṭā); a streak, stripe, broad line; a kind of bracelet; half the beard or one side of the face; a leaf of the American aloe or of any similar plant; the head-stall of a bridle; a long narrow slip or strip of coarse cloth, matting, &c.
subs: s.m. One who plays with foils or cudgels, a fencer; a man who fights with sword and shield; — (met.), coquette, wanton, strumpet
s.f. Playing with foils or cudgels, fencing
v.n. To practice with foils, to fence.

def: s.m. Winged or flying animal; a bird.
s.f. = paṭah, q.v.
s.m. Drum, kettle-drum.
s.f. A small shrub, Abrus precatorius.
s.m. Straw or grass spread over a field to protect the fresh-planted roots from the sun.
s.f. Collection of leaves (esp. of the sugar-cane).
s.m. Lease-holder; a village priest.
s.m. One who practises with foils, &c.; fencer; cudgel-player; — a lout, a blockhead.
s.m. That which causes to fall or sink; sin, crime, guilt; — uncleanness, impurity, defilement, pollution
subs: v.n. To be touched with anything unclean, be defiled or polluted; to be stained or sullied (the character).

def: s.m. Letter-bearer, messenger.
s.m. A loud sound, a bursting sound, a crash
subs: adv. With a crash, loud, loudly.

def: s.m. Throwing, dashing down; throw, dash, fall (see paṭaknā and paṭkan).
s.m. Cotton cloth; camp, encampment.
s.m. = S پتاکا पताका patākā, s.f. Flag, banner; flag-staff; emblem carried as an ensign or banner, symbol, sign, mark; a particular high number; an episode in a drama.
s.f. Flag, banner.
s.f. = S پتاك पताक patāk, s.m. Flag, banner; flag-staff; emblem carried as an ensign or banner, symbol, sign, mark; a particular high number; an episode in a drama.
s.m. Cracker; squib; a gun-cap.
s.m. = paṭākā, q.v. s.f. = S پتاك पताक patāk, s.m. Flag, banner; flag-staff; emblem carried as an ensign or banner, symbol, sign, mark; a particular high number; an episode in a drama.
wety: Hyperforeign form of پَٹاکا (paṭākā). Displaced پَٹاکا (paṭākā). Onomatopoeic, from پٹ (paṭ, “crack”).

def: s.m. Cracker; squib; a gun-cap.

v.t. and n. = paṭaknā, q.v. v.t. To dash against, to knock down, dash down, to throw (one) down with violence; to throw about (the arms and legs) convulsively; — v.n. To abate, go down, or subside (a swelling or tumour); to crack, crackle (see paṭ)
adj. & s.m. Fallen, sinful, guilty, criminal; — sinner, criminal.
s.m. Ensign, standard-bearer; one who gives a signal.

ety: S. पात, rt. पत्+अक+अनीयं; see paṛnā
def: v.t. To dash against, to knock down, dash down, to throw (one) down with violence; to throw about (the arms and legs) convulsively; — v.n. To abate, go down, or subside (a swelling or tumour); to crack, crackle (see paṭ)
s.m. Letter-bearer, messenger.

s.m. One of the seven regions under the earth and the abode of the Nāgas or serpents and demons; hell, the infernal regions; — hole, chasm, excavation; submarine fire; — a sort of apparatus for calcining and subliming metals (formed of two earthen pots, the upper one inverted over the lower, and the two joined together by their necks with cement and placed in a hole containing fire)
subs: s.f. The Ganges of the infernal regions.

def: adj. & s.m. Pale-red, of a pink or rose colour; — pale-red hue, pink, rose-colour, redness; — the trumpet flower, Bignonia suaveolens; — a species of rice that ripens in the rains

s.m. Roof, thatch; cover, covering, veil; a film over the eye; a basket; heap, mass, number, quantity, multitude; retinue, train; book, division, section, chapter (of a book); a formulary (of ritual) worship; a sandal-wood mark on the forehead.
s.f. The trumpet flower, Bignonia suaveolens.
s.f. = pāṭalā, q.v.; — name of a city
subs: s.m. Name of the capital of Magadh (near the confluence of the Son and Ganges), supposed to be the ancient Palibothra and the modern Patna.

def: adj. Closed, shut (the eyes)
subs: v.n. To be closed (the eyes), to be blind ( = paṭ ho-jānā).

def: s.m. See s.v. pāṭ. s.m. Falling; sin, wickedness.
s.m. = pāṭ-ambar, q.v. s.m. Falling; sin, wickedness.
s.f. Shoe, slipper.
s.m. (Braj) pl. of pāṭ, 'slab' &c., q.v.
s.f. Roofing; roof, &c. (see pāṭnā); — city, mart; name of the city of Patna.
s.m. Falling; fall; falling from dignity, virtue, &c.; hanging down, becoming slack; death.
s.m. Roofing, &c. (see pāṭan and paṭnā).

ety: caus. of paṭnā, q.v.; and see also pāṭnā
def: v.t. To cause to be boarded, roofed, or covered; to place the beams on the roof of a house; to board (a room); to water, irrigate (a field); to prepare a ćaukā with cowdung or earth dissolved in water; to fill up, make level (a hole, &c.); to realize the amount (of a bill, &c.); to pay money, settle (an account), conclude (a bargain).
s.m. Sappan wood, &c. = patrāng, q.v.s.v. patra.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पतङ्ग (pataṅga, “the flier, flying insect e.g. moth”).

def: s.m. A bird; a flying insect, grasshopper, moth; a paper kite (syn. kankauwā); the sun; a ball for playing with (syn. geṅd); a name of Kr̤ishṇa
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit पतङ्ग (pataṅga, “the flier, flying insect e.g. moth”).

def: s.m. A flying insect; moth; grasshopper; a spark (of fire), a live coal
subs: v.n. To move about briskly, go rapidly about a business.

def: s.f. Name of one of the wives of Tārksha, and mother of the patagas or flying animals.
s.f. A small bird; a sort of bee.
adj. & s.m. Composed by Patanjali; — the Yoga system of philosophy (first taught by Patanjali).
s.m. Name of a saint or muni, teacher of the Yoga philosophy (thence called pātanjala); name of the celebrated author of the Mahā-bhāshya or great commentary on Pāṇini.
s.m. Roofing (a house with tiles, &c.); covering (a floor, &c.) with boards; apparatus for roofing or covering (such as) beams, rafters, planks, &c.; watering, or flooding (a field), irrigation.
s.m. A kind of fruit.
s.m. see s.v. paṭ, 'Sound of beating.' s.m. Falling; sin, wickedness.
s.m. Moth, butterfly.
s.f. Prostitute; dancing-girl
adj. Spare, emaciate, thin, lean; weak ( = patlā).
s.f. = pātar, pātariyā, qq.v. s.f. Prostitute; dancing-girl
s.f. lit. 'Winged'; a bird; an arrow.
s.m. = pāṭaunī; paṭnī; qq.v.
s.m. A leaf.
adj. & s.m. Clever, sharp, dexterous; — sharpness, acuteness, intensity, energy; cleverness, skill, dexterity, talent; quickness, precipitation.
adj. Clever, adroit, dexterous; cunning, crafty, fraudulent.
s.m. Mortgage; pledge; a pledge of which the usufruct pays both the principal and interest within a definite period (and thus ensures its own redemption).
s.m. A fire-fly (syn. jugnū; shab-tāb).
s.m. Fencing with the paṭā; cudgelling
subs: (-meṅ, or -par), To interrupt, to throw obstacles (in the way of); to cudgel, to beat.

def: s.m. Sovereignty; a chief among the Kumbī tribe; a Kumbī; the headman of a village, a title of Marhaṭṭās.

s.m. = paṭerā, q.v. s.m. The plant Cyperus hexastachyus communis; the reed or flag Cyperus papyrus; a long coarse grass (used as fodder for elephants, and made into mats, and rain-cloaks or hoods for the poorer classes).
s.m. A stone, slab, slate; a log or plank used as a harrow; a large flat-bottomed boat.
s.f. A small flat-bottomed boat.
s.m. = paṭīnā, q.v. s.m. A species of bird, the green fly-catcher, Merops viridis.

s.m. The plant Cyperus hexastachyus communis; the reed or flag Cyperus papyrus; a long coarse grass (used as fodder for elephants, and made into mats, and rain-cloaks or hoods for the poorer classes).

s.m. The plant Cyperus hexastachyus communis; the reed or flag Cyperus papyrus; a long coarse grass (used as fodder for elephants, and made into mats, and rain-cloaks or hoods for the poorer classes).
s.m. Water ( = jal).
s.m. Path, course, way, road, highway
subs: s.m. Travelling expenses; provisions for a journey
s.m. 'Way-showing, or shower'; guide, leader, director, conductor
s.m. One who guards a way.

def: s.m. Proper, fit, or wholesome food (for a patient), diet, regimen; spoon-diet, broth, soup; — syrup, sherbet (esp. as a regimen).
s.m. A young full-grown animal; a youth in his prime, a well-developed youth; a wrestler; a young tree which has not yet borne fruit ( = paṭṭhā; pāṭhā).
s.m. Reading, perusal, study, recital (esp. sacred study); text of reading; lecture; lesson, task
subs: s.f. Place where the Vedas are read or studied
pāṭh karnā, v.n. To read, study; to repeat or revise a lesson; to recite or repeat daily portions of the Vedas, &c.
pāṭh-sālā, s.f. Reading-room, lecture-hall, school-house, college, primary (Hindī) school
pāṭh manjarī, s.f. A particular small bird, Graculus religiosa
s.m. Reading-master; professor of elocution; preceptor, teacher, schoolmaster

def: s.m. Young, full-grown animal (commonly a goat, deer, or an elephant; syn. paṭh, paṭṭhā); a robust youth; a young wrestler.
part. = paṭhāyā, perf. part. of paṭhānā.

s.m. Reader, lecturer, preceptor, tutor, teacher, professor; spiritual preceptor; a Brāhman who reads in public the Purāṇas or other sacred works of the Hindūs; a panḍit who declares what is the law or custom according to the sacred writings; a title of Brāhmans.
s.m. (f. -ikā), Reader, reciter.
s.m. Bdellium.
s.m. Reading; lecturing, teaching.

s.m. Name of a race inhabiting the hilly country to the north-west of Lahore (syn. Afg̠ān); name of a race or dynasty that reigned in Dehli; name of a tribe of Mohammadans (descended from the paṭhān or Afg̠hān race, who settled in India, particularly in Ruhelkhand); soldier, warrior, man-at-arms.
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀧𑀝𑁆𑀞𑀸𑀡 (*paṭṭhāṇa), from Proto-Middle Indo-Aryan *𑀧𑀱𑁆𑀝𑀸𑀦 (*paṣṭāna), borrowed from Proto-Iranian. Compare Punjabi پَٹھاݨ (paṭhāṇ), Odia ପଠାଣ (paṭhāṇa), Gujarati પઠાણ (paṭhāṇ), Marathi पठाण (paṭhāṇ), Bengali পাঠান (paṭhan). Related to Pashto پښتانۀ (paẍtânë); see there for more.

def: s.m. Reading, reciting; mentioning; reader, student.

ety: paṭhā˚ = paṭhāw˚ = Prk. पट्ठावे(इ), or पट्ठाव(इ) = S. प्रस्थापय(ति), caus. of rt. प्र+स्था
def: v.t. To send, despatch ( = bhejnā).
s.f. A woman of the paṭhān race; the wife of a paṭhān.
v.t. = paṭhānā, q.v. v.t. To send, despatch ( = bhejnā).

s.m. Stone = patthar, q.v. s.m. Stone, rock; precious stone, gem; hailstone; anything hard, troublesome, trying, intolerable, &c.; — adj. Hard; hard-hearted; heavy; dull; difficult; disagreeable, intolerable, &c.
subs: s.m. One who plays at stone-throwing; one who is fond of, or is addicted to, throwiug stones; — patthar-bāzī, s.f. Stone-throwing; fondness for stone-throwing
v.n. The melting of a stony heart; a difficult task to be facilitated
(-par), Stones to fall (upon); to hail; to be crushed under a shower of stones or hailstones; to be overwhelmed with trouble, &c.; to be injured, ruined or destroyed
v.n. A stony heart to be softened or melted
s.m. A breaker of stones; — the woodpecker, Picus
(-par), To throw stones (at), to pelt with stones
patthar-tale hāth ānā or dabnā = patthar tale dāman dabnā
v.n. To be entangled in difficulties
s.m. One who is attached to the very stones of his house and who clings to it even in distress; a skinflint; a kind of serpent; a sort of fish; a sort of pot-herb
v.t. To put (a knife, &c.), to the grindstone or hone; to whet or sharpen (a knife, &c.)
(or ćhātī par patthar rakhnā), v.n. To be patient by compulsion, or from being without remedy, to suffer patiently
v.n. lit. 'To carry stones'; to labour hard
v.t. To give a rude or sharp answer; to reply rashly without understanding the meaning of one's question; to give a blunt refusal
v.t. lit. 'To break one's own head with a stone'; to teach or instruct a fool
s.m. Stony or hard heart; hard-heartedness
adj. lit. 'A line engraved on stone'; ineffaceable, ineradicable, unalterable

v.n. To become stone, to be petrified ( = pathrānā); to become hard as stone, to become rigid; to be heavy or burdensome; to be unmerciful; to receive no impression, to be impervious (to); to be fixed or immovable, to be still as a statue, be stable, stand still; to freeze, to congeal; to be stone-blind; to become useless or unserviceable.

def: s.m. Water ( = pāth)
subs: pātho-nidhi, s.m. lit. 'Receptacle, or treasure-house, of waters'; the ocean.

def: s.m. Provender or provisions for a journey, viaticum.

adj. & s.m. Knowing, conversant with; — a Brāhman who has finished his studies; one who has read or studied (any subject), student; one who gives lessons, teacher, professor; — the plant Plumbago zeylanica; — a kind of fish.
s.m. The tree Acacia catechu; or Mimosa alba.
s.m. (f. -ā and -ī), Traveller, wayfarer, passenger, sojourner; guide, one who knows the way
subs: pathik-gr̤iha, s.m. Refuge or resting-place for travellers, halting-place, waiting-place, inn, caravansary
adj. Unfrequented, untravelled, deserted (as a road, or a caravansary).

def: s.f. Female student; female lecturer.
s.m. = pāṭhak, q.v.; — a kind of sheat-fish, Silurus pelorius or Boalis.
s.m. Road, &c. = path, q.v.; one of the divisions of hell. s.m. Water ( = jal).
part. Instructed, taught, lectured.
part. Read, studied, recited.
s.f. A young full-grown animal; a young she-goat, &c.
s.m. Courier or messenger sent from place to place.

ety: S. स्थापनीयं per met.; cf. H. thāpnā
def: v.t. To make up cowdung into cakes for fuel.
v.t. To read, &c. = paṛhnā, q.v. v.t. To read, repeat, recite; to say; to mutter a spell; to learn, study (with acc. & abl.); to make out, decipher; to talk (as a bird)
subs: adj. Fit to be read; what can be read, readable, legible
paṛhne-hārā, s.m. (f. -ī), Reader, reciter; learner, student.

def: v.n. To be made into cakes (cowdung).
s.m. Young (of an animal), kid; lamb; chicken, &c.

s.m. = paṭrā, q.v. s.m. & adj. Vessel (in general), goblet, bowl, cup, plate, dish, jar, pot; utensil; receptacle, recipient, repository, proper object; what holds or supports; king's counsellor or minister; the bed of a river; a leaf; — capable, worthy, fit, eligible, deserving, able; accomplished (used in comp.).
v.n. To be petrified; to become hard, to indurate, &c. ( = patthar honā, q.v.s.v. patthar).
s.m. Stoning
s.f. (dim.) [S. प्रस्तर+वट+कः], A bowl of stone.
s.f. (dim.) = H پتهروٿا पथरौटा pathrauṭā, s.m. [S. प्रस्तर+वट+कः], A bowl of stone.
adj. (f. -ī), Stony, rocky; gravelly; gritty.
adj. (f. -ī), Stony, rocky; gravelly; gritty.
v.t. To cause (one) to be sent; to send ( = paṭhānā).
s.f. Place where cowdung is patted into cakes.
part. adj. To be read, perused or studied, worth reading; to be taught, needing instruction; legible, admitting of being read.
s.f. The tree Terminalia chebula, or T. citrina; — a species of metre in Sanskrit poetry.
adj. & s.m. Belonging to a road; furthering, helping; agreeing with, proper, fit, suitable, wholesome (used in a medical sense of diet, regimen, &c); — wholesome diet, &c. = path, q.v.
s.m. = pati; and pāṅti; qq.v.
s.f. Leaf; leaf on which anything has been written, letter, note, epistle, document, &c.; sign, trace, clue ( = patā).
s.f. Side-pieces of a bedstead; a kind of board on which children learn to write; a kind of mat ( = sītal pāṭi); parting of the hair in the middle of the head; a kind of sweetmeat.
s.m. = pāṭin, q.v. s.m. A species of fish (described as having many teeth).
s.f. Arithmetic.
s.m. See پت pati, 'Master.'
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀆 (pattiā), from Sanskrit पत्त्रिका (pattrikā), from पत्त्र (pattra) + -इका (-ikā).

def: s.f. The curtain of a stage.
s.m. Master, lord, owner, proprietor, governor; husband
subs: s.m. The disposition and sentiments appropriate in a wife towards her husband
s.m. The status of a master, or lord, or husband
s.f. A faithful wife (ʻwhose husband is her life')
s.f. Devotion of a wife to her husband
s.f. A woman who murders her husband, husband-killer; a line on the hand indicating that a woman will be faithless or treacherous to her husband
pati-vantī, pati-vatnī, s.f. A woman who possesses a husband, a married woman, a wife whose husband is living
vulg. pati-brat, s.m. Loyalty or fidelity to a husband; chastity, modesty
vulg. pati-bratā, pati-birtā, pati-bhartā, s.f. Faithful, virtuous, and devoted wife

def: s.m. See s.v. pati.
adv. = prati-din, q.v. adv. From day to day, day by day, every day, daily; always, continually.

s.m. = prati-grah, q.v. s.m. Receiving, accepting, acceptance, receiving a donation; one who receives, receiver; a gift, present; a proper donation or fit present to a Brāhman at suitable periods; the right of accepting gifts (as the peculiar prerogative of Brāhmans); friendly reception; the reserve of an army; a spitting-pot, spittoon; the sun near the moon's node.
s.m. The Gangetic porpoise, Delphinus gangeticus (syn. sūṅs).
adj. Thin, &c. = patlā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Spare, emaciate, lean, delicate, weak, feeble; fine, attenuated, thin (cloth, paper, or liquid), slender; sharp, tapering; rarefied, subtle
subs: s.f. A thin thing; a subtle substance; a fluid body
s.m. Straitened circumstances, bad plight or condition
v.t. To make thin, &c.; to sharpen (a pencil, &c.); to rarefy, to liquefy.

def: s.m. Match, or wick, for a lamp, candle-wick; a linstock (also written fatīl)
s.m. = patīla, q.v.
s.m. A wide-mouthed saucepan or cauldron ( = patīlā).
s.m. A wide-mouthed saucepan or cauldron ( = patīlā).
s.m. A copper pot or pan with a wide mouth, a cauldron
s.m. A copper pot or pan with a wide mouth, a cauldron
s.f. A kind of small earthen pot used by religious mendicants; ( = patīlī, patīla, qq.v.).
s.f. A small pot or pan.
v.t. To wring from, extort, exact; to subdue; to capture, catch; to win, gain, net; to cheat, cozen; to cudgel, beat
s.m. The plank on which cloth is spread for printing.
s.f. A woman who chooses her husband for herself; — the plant Nigella Indica.
s.m. A species of fish (described as having many teeth).
s.m. A species of bird, the green fly-catcher, Merops viridis.
s.m. Sandal; a pungent root, a kind of radish.
s.f. A kind of mat.
part. Lowered, humbled, overthrown; depressed, downcast.
part. adj. & s.m. (f. ā), Fallen, abject, degraded, abandoned, outcast, guilty, wicked; fallow (land); — abject or degraded person, outcast; guilty person, sinner
subs: adj. Purifying the guilty, an epithet of the Deity.

def: s.m. Fallow land, uncultivated or waste land
subs: s.f. Reduction or decrease occasioned by lands being left uncultivated.

def: s.f. = patitwa, q.v. s.m. Mastership, proprietorship, lordship; the marital or conjugal state, matrimony.
s.m. Mastership, proprietorship, lordship; the marital or conjugal state, matrimony.
= S پتورتا पतिव्रता pati-vratā, See s.v. pati.
= S پتورت पतिव्रत pati-vrat, See s.v. pati.
s.f. A slip of board (used as a slate for writing on = paṭṭī), tablet, slab of stone, slate.
s.m. Piling a heap of paṭṭīs or writing boards on a boy's back (a punishment resorted to in Hindī schools).
s.f. patwār, q.v.
s.m. Belief, confidence, trust, reliance, dependence.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. pat, 'leaf.' s.m. Falling; sin, wickedness.
s.m. A cloth worn round the waist, girdle, sash, belt; a layer of mortar in a brick wall
subs: (-par), To gird up the loins (for), to prepare (for), or determine (on, an act, enterprise, &c.)
(-kā), 'To seize the belt (of); to hinder, hold back, prevent, obstruct.

def: = paṭkā, q.v.
s.f. A knock-down, a fall; a sharp blow; prostration, after-effects (of a disease, &c.); disquiet, restlessness, uneasiness
subs: v.n. To be thrown, to fall on the ground with force (see next).

def: s.m. A throw or fall (in wrestling &c.); a knock-down
subs: (-ko), paṭkanā sunānā, To dash (one) down, to give a violent fall (in wrestling)
v.n. To have a severe fall, be thrown violently (in wrestling); to be knocked down.

def: v.t. = paṭaknā, q.v. v.t. To dash against, to knock down, dash down, to throw (one) down with violence; to throw about (the arms and legs) convulsively; — v.n. To abate, go down, or subside (a swelling or tumour); to crack, crackle (see paṭ)
(dim.) s.f. = H پٿکهنا पटखना paṭkhanā, s.m. = paṭkanā, q.v.
s.m. = H پٿکاري पटकारी paṭkārī, s.f. = phaṭkār, q.v.
s.f. = H پٿکار पटकार paṭkār, s.m. = phaṭkār, q.v.

v.t. = paṭaknā, q.v. v.t. To dash against, to knock down, dash down, to throw (one) down with violence; to throw about (the arms and legs) convulsively; — v.n. To abate, go down, or subside (a swelling or tumour); to crack, crackle (see paṭ)
s.m. = H پٿکني पटकनी paṭkanī, (dim.) s.f. = paṭkanā, q.v.
s.f. Fall, &c. = paṭaknī; paṭkan
subs: v.t. To throw down, &c. = paṭaknā denā.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Spare, emaciate, lean, delicate, weak, feeble; fine, attenuated, thin (cloth, paper, or liquid), slender; sharp, tapering; rarefied, subtle
subs: s.f. A thin thing; a subtle substance; a fluid body
s.m. Straitened circumstances, bad plight or condition
v.t. To make thin, &c.; to sharpen (a pencil, &c.); to rarefy, to liquefy.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀧𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀮 (pattala, “thin”), from Sanskrit पत्त्रल (pattralá, “leafy”). Cognate with Gujarati પાતળું (pātaḷũ), Marathi पातळ (pātaḷ), Odia ପାତଳ (pātaḷa), Bengali পাতলা (patola), Assamese পাতল (patol).

def: s.f. = patlā-pan, q.v. s.m. Leanness, emaciation, thinness, fineness, attenuation, minuteness, meagreness, delicateness, weakness; wateriness; subtleness ( = patlāʼī).
v.t. = patlā karnā, q.v.s.v. patlā. adj. (f. -ī), Spare, emaciate, lean, delicate, weak, feeble; fine, attenuated, thin (cloth, paper, or liquid), slender; sharp, tapering; rarefied, subtle
subs: s.f. A thin thing; a subtle substance; a fluid body
s.m. Straitened circumstances, bad plight or condition
v.t. To make thin, &c.; to sharpen (a pencil, &c.); to rarefy, to liquefy.

def: s.m. Leanness, emaciation, thinness, fineness, attenuation, minuteness, meagreness, delicateness, weakness; wateriness; subtleness ( = patlāʼī).
s.m. Dead leaves fallen from a tree; leaves cut off from the culm of the reed Saccharum spontaneum and used for thatching.
s.f. Sowing fresh sugar-cane after cutting the old.

ety: paṭ˚ = Prk. पट्टे(इ), or पट(इ), fr. S. पट्ट 'a roof'; and पत्र 'a vessel'
def: v.n. To be covered, be roofed, be thatched, be boarded; to fill, be filled up or closed, be made level; to be watered or irrigated (fields, &c.); to be full, be piled up, to abound, swarm, be thronged or crowded, be glutted (a market): cf. paṭānā, and pāṭnā

ety: paṭ˚ = Prk. पट्टे(इ), or पट्ट(इ), fr. S. पट्ट
def: v.n. To be paid or discharged, be repaid or remitted; to be procured; to be accepted or honoured (a bill); to be admitted, passed, received as good; to do well, flourish, be carried on satisfactorily, be executed creditably or satisfactorily (a business); to agree, to be closed or struck (a bargain)
s.m. Ora vulvœ.

v.t. To lay planks, &c. (across), to board (a floor); to roof, cover, shut or close in; to fill, fill up (a well, hole, ditch, &c.); to overstock, heap, pile, accumulate; — to water, irrigate; to pay.
s.f. (of pati), A wife
subs: s.m. Division of property among a man's widows.

def: s.m. Boatman, waterman, ferryman ( = pāṭūnī, pāṭaunī).
s.f. Son's wife daughter-in-law.
s.m. An owl ( = ullū).
s.f. Son's wife daughter-in-law.
s.m. = S پٿولکا पटोलिका paṭolikā, s.f. A species of cucumber, Trichosanthis diœca (syn. palwal).
s.f. = S پتول पटोल paṭol, s.m. A species of cucumber, Trichosanthis diœca (syn. palwal).
s.m. = pāṭal-opal, q.v.s.v. pāṭal.
s.m. Roofing with planks or boards, transom.
adj. & s.m. = paṭpaṛ, q.v.
= H پٿپڙ पटपड़ paṭ-paṛ, adj. & s.m., see s.v. paṭ, 'lying flat.'
= H پٿپر पटपर paṭ-par, adj. & s.m., see s.v. paṭ, 'lying flat.'
s.f. A kind of small bird, the grass-warbler.

s.m. & adj. Vessel (in general), goblet, bowl, cup, plate, dish, jar, pot; utensil; receptacle, recipient, repository, proper object; what holds or supports; king's counsellor or minister; the bed of a river; a leaf; — capable, worthy, fit, eligible, deserving, able; accomplished (used in comp.).

ety: S. पत्र, or पट्ट + dim. aff. रः
def: s.m. A plank; a plank with low side supports (to sit on); a plank on which a washerman beats clothes; a long thick plank used as a harrow; a board on which flour is kneaded, &c.; — unripe grain, Cicer arietinum
subs: (-par), To pass the paṭṛā (over land), to harrow
v.t. To make level, to level, raze, demolish, destroy, lay waste; to deprive of power or strength, render helpless or powerless; to convict (an adversary) and leave him without reply.

ety: S. पत्र, or पट्ट + dim. aff. रः
def: s.m. A plank; a plank with low side supports (to sit on); a plank on which a washerman beats clothes; a long thick plank used as a harrow; a board on which flour is kneaded, &c.; — unripe grain, Cicer arietinum
subs: (-par), To pass the paṭṛā (over land), to harrow
v.t. To make level, to level, raze, demolish, destroy, lay waste; to deprive of power or strength, render helpless or powerless; to convict (an adversary) and leave him without reply.

def: s.m. = patrāng, q.v.s.v. patra.
s.m. = S پترانك पत्रांक patrāṅk, s.m. See s.v. patra, patr.
s.m. = S پترانگ पत्रांग patrāṅg, s.m. See s.v. patra, patr.
s.f. (masc. pātratwa), The condition of being a receptacle or a recipient; capacity, fitness, propriety, worthiness, dignity.
adj. & s.m. Drinking; drinker; — protecting, nourishing; protector, &c.
s.f. Letter, note, epistle (see patra).
adj. & s.m. Winged, feathered; — a bird; an arrow; a person riding on any vehicle; a mountain.

s.f. A narrow slip of wood or metal; a board, slate or slab (for writing on; syn. paṭiyā); the vanes of a Venetian window; a tile; the banks of a canal, &c.; an avenue; a firm seat on horseback; an armlet or bracelet of brass, gilt, gold, or silver.

s.f. A narrow slip of wood or metal; a board, slate or slab (for writing on; syn. paṭiyā); the vanes of a Venetian window; a tile; the banks of a canal, &c.; an avenue; a firm seat on horseback; an armlet or bracelet of brass, gilt, gold, or silver.

s.f. Leaf; written leaf; page; letter, document ( = patra, q.v.). s.m. & adj. Vessel (in general), goblet, bowl, cup, plate, dish, jar, pot; utensil; receptacle, recipient, repository, proper object; what holds or supports; king's counsellor or minister; the bed of a river; a leaf; — capable, worthy, fit, eligible, deserving, able; accomplished (used in comp.).
s.m. A kind of bird, the fly-catcher, Merops viridis; the fork-tailed shrike, or king-crow (syn. bhujang).
s.m. The plant Ocymum pilosum.
= S پترورنه पत्रोर्ण patrorṇa, See s.v. patra, patr.
= S پتروپسکر पत्रोपस्कर patropaskar, See s.v. patra, patr.
s.m. = pāṭh-śālā, q.v.s.v. pāṭh.
s.m. The plant Crotolaria juncea.

ety: S. पत्त्त्र+कः; Prk. पत्तं, or पत्तओ
def: s.m. Leaf (of a tree); a card; an ornament worn in the upper part of the ear; a leaf-shaped ornament hanging from an ear-ring
subs: v.n. To run as fast as one can, to take to one's heels (so called from a boy's game in which the runner has to pluck a leaf from a certain tree and catch one of the rest who have run off)
v.n. To run off, run away, flee.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit पत्तिअ (pattia), from Sanskrit प्रत्याययति (pratyāyayati, “convinces”).

def: s.m. A board on which Hindūs sit whilst eating their meals or performing religious ceremonies; a fuller's board, board, table; seat; a lock of hair, ringlet, curl; the collar of a horse, or of a dog, &c.; girth of a horse; shoulder-strap and badge (of a peon, &c.); gold or silver threads of an old native shoe; an ornament (worn by women as an appendage to the bālī); an ornament for the forehead, frontlet; a deed (com. a title-deed to land), a deed of lease; a code or book of regulations for the guidance of rent-collectors
subs: (-kā), To take off the strap and badge, to dismiss from service
s.m. Exchange of seats by the bride and bridegroom at the conclusion of a (Hindu) marriage ceremony
v.n. 'To break the collar and go off'; to get loose, to run away
paṭṭā ṭheke-dārī, s.m. A farming lease
s.m. Lease granted to tenants individually
s.m. Granter of a lease, lessor
s.m. Fine, fee, or complimentary present of money, on the grant of a lease
adj. & adv. According to shares or assessment.

def: s.m. = paṭṭā, q.v. s.m. Leaf (of a tree); a card; an ornament worn in the upper part of the ear; a leaf-shaped ornament hanging from an ear-ring
subs: v.n. To run as fast as one can, to take to one's heels (so called from a boy's game in which the runner has to pluck a leaf from a certain tree and catch one of the rest who have run off)
v.n. To run off, run away, flee.

def: s.m. Slab, tablet (for painting); plate (of metal, for inscription or engraving of royal edicts, grants, &c.); patent, document, royal grant or order (written on copper, stone, &c); seat, chair, throne; a strip; frontlet; turban, tiara, diadem; woven silk; an upper or outer garment; bandage, ligature, &c.; city, town, village; — the plant Corchorus olitorius (from the fibres of the bark of which, called jute, a coarse sackcloth and cordage are prepared)
subs: paṭṭaj, s.m. A kind of canvas or sackcloth
paṭṭ-rānī, s.f. The principal queen, queen-consort ( = paṭ-rānī; pāt-rānī)
s.m. The plant Cœsalpina sappan (used in dyeing)

def: s.m. See s.v. paṭṭa. s.m. Leaf (of a tree); a card; an ornament worn in the upper part of the ear; a leaf-shaped ornament hanging from an ear-ring
subs: v.n. To run as fast as one can, to take to one's heels (so called from a boy's game in which the runner has to pluck a leaf from a certain tree and catch one of the rest who have run off)
v.n. To run off, run away, flee.

ety: irr. Ar. pl. of paṭṭa = paṭṭā
def: s.m. Deeds of lease, title-deeds to land.
s.m. Document; bandage; frontlet (see paṭṭa and paṭṭā).
s.f. A plate or trencher made of leaves; a plate of cakes, sweetmeats, &c.
subs: v.n. To withhold or bar the meal or repast (this is done till one of the guests shall have solved the riddle which is proposed by one of the women of the party)
v.n. To open the repast, commence the meal (on the solution of the proposed riddle).

def: s.m. = pātāl, q.v. s.m. One of the seven regions under the earth and the abode of the Nāgas or serpents and demons; hell, the infernal regions; — hole, chasm, excavation; submarine fire; — a sort of apparatus for calcining and subliming metals (formed of two earthen pots, the upper one inverted over the lower, and the two joined together by their necks with cement and placed in a hole containing fire)
subs: s.f. The Ganges of the infernal regions.

def: s.m. City, town, settlement, colony (chiefly used, like ābād, in comp., e.g. Srīrangpattan, 'Seringapatam').
s.m. The act of ordering goods from a manufacturer (cf. paṭnā).
s.m. City ( = pattan, and used chiefly at the end of compounds).
s.m. = pattan, q.v. s.m. City, town, settlement, colony (chiefly used, like ābād, in comp., e.g. Srīrangpattan, 'Seringapatam').
adj. Commissioned, manufactured by order.
s.f. = pattanī, q.v. adj. Commissioned, manufactured by order.

s.m. = patthar, q.v. s.m. Stone, rock; precious stone, gem; hailstone; anything hard, troublesome, trying, intolerable, &c.; — adj. Hard; hard-hearted; heavy; dull; difficult; disagreeable, intolerable, &c.
subs: s.m. One who plays at stone-throwing; one who is fond of, or is addicted to, throwiug stones; — patthar-bāzī, s.f. Stone-throwing; fondness for stone-throwing
v.n. The melting of a stony heart; a difficult task to be facilitated
(-par), Stones to fall (upon); to hail; to be crushed under a shower of stones or hailstones; to be overwhelmed with trouble, &c.; to be injured, ruined or destroyed
v.n. A stony heart to be softened or melted
s.m. A breaker of stones; — the woodpecker, Picus
(-par), To throw stones (at), to pelt with stones
patthar-tale hāth ānā or dabnā = patthar tale dāman dabnā
v.n. To be entangled in difficulties
s.m. One who is attached to the very stones of his house and who clings to it even in distress; a skinflint; a kind of serpent; a sort of fish; a sort of pot-herb
v.t. To put (a knife, &c.), to the grindstone or hone; to whet or sharpen (a knife, &c.)
(or ćhātī par patthar rakhnā), v.n. To be patient by compulsion, or from being without remedy, to suffer patiently
v.n. lit. 'To carry stones'; to labour hard
v.t. To give a rude or sharp answer; to reply rashly without understanding the meaning of one's question; to give a blunt refusal
v.t. lit. 'To break one's own head with a stone'; to teach or instruct a fool
s.m. Stony or hard heart; hard-heartedness
adj. lit. 'A line engraved on stone'; ineffaceable, ineradicable, unalterable

v.n. To become stone, to be petrified ( = pathrānā); to become hard as stone, to become rigid; to be heavy or burdensome; to be unmerciful; to receive no impression, to be impervious (to); to be fixed or immovable, to be still as a statue, be stable, stand still; to freeze, to congeal; to be stone-blind; to become useless or unserviceable.

def: s.m. = paṭh, q.v. s.m. Water ( = jal).

s.m. A blade; a long pointed leaf; a nerve sinew, tendon; stiff cover of a book; lace; a bracelet or bangle; a lock of hair on the side of the head; the parasitic plant Loranthus longiflorus; the plant Viscum attenuatum; the shrub Kydia calycina, or K. fraterna.

wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀢𑁆𑀣𑀭 m (patthara, “stone”) without cluster simplification, from Sanskrit प्रस्तर (prastara). Displaced Old Hindi पाथर (pāthara). Cognate with Punjabi پَتَّھر (pattahr). False cognate of Ancient Greek πέτρα (pétra).

def: s.m. A kind of stone weight.
s.f. Grit, gravel, stone; a flint; hone, whetstone; stone or gravel in the bladder, calculus; a gizzard.
s.m. Pedestrian; foot-soldier; — s.f. The smallest division of an army, company, platoon (consisting of one chariot, one elephant, three horsemen, and five foot-soldiers)
s.f. Leaf; leaves; hemp-leaves (of which an intoxicating potion is made); a leaf or thin plate (of metal); a hem; a share ( = paṭṭī)
subs: (-kī), To beat off the leaves (of trees)
v.n. To shed its leaves (a tree)
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀧𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀆 (pattiā), from Sanskrit पत्त्रिका (pattrikā), from पत्त्र (pattra) + -इका (-ikā).

def: s.f. A piece or strip of cloth; bandage, ligature, swath, plaster, cataplasm; splints, band, fillet, ribbon, tape, brace, tie; head-band, a narrow plate of metal (worn across the turban by sepoys and their native officers); a rafter, or the whole row or series of rafters that cover one side of a house; side piece of the frame of a bedstead ( = pāṭī); parting of the hair in the middle; side-locks of hair over each temple; lath (of split bamboo, &c.); spokes of a wheel; a ledge; line, row; a rule to draw lines by; a small plank, board, tablet, table (as in 'plane-table'); a small rectangular board on which children write ( = pāṭī); a morsel of betel-leaf made up with betel-nut, lime, &c. ( = bīṛī; pān); a foil, &c. ( = paṭā); piece, part, portion; division of a village; division of land into portions or strips; holding or tenure in coparcenary; a written order or patent; a title to a certain tenure of land; list, statement, invoice, document (of any kind); public subscription paper; cess, assessment; — a kind of sweetmeat ( = pāṭī)
subs: (-par), To apply a bandage, &c. (to a sore or wound); to bandage (the eyes), to blindfold

v.t. To teach the letters (to a child) from the paṭṭī or board; (fig.) to explain (anything to anyone), to give a lesson (to); to advise, counsel; to put (one) up to (some trick or roguery); to cheat, deceive, take in
A phrase used to denote either excessive weakness or excessive laziness
s.m. Holder or proprietor of a paṭṭī or share in a coparcenary village or estate, a coparcener
s.f. Coparcenary tenure; an estate held in severalty
s.f. Coparcenary tenure where part of the land is divided and part held in common; tenure of an estate in partial severalty
adj. & adv. According to shares, holdings, assessment, &c.
(-par), To paste the hair down (on the temples).

def: s.f. = pāṭh, q.v. s.m. Water ( = jal).
s.f. A ribbon; a turban-cloth (cf. paṭṭī).
s.m. The plant Cœsalpina bonducella.
